title: Ultimate Moving Checklist from Top Movers
excerpt: Phased checklist from eight weeks through first week in the new home: utilities, IDs, packing, moving day, destination, paperwork, contingencies.
content:
Ultimate moving checklist from top movers

Work top to bottom. Earlier phases unlock later ones. Share this list with household members and your crew lead.

Eight weeks before

  • Research movers; read reviews with specifics.
  • Declutter; sell or donate bulky items.
  • Request three written estimates on identical scope.

Six weeks before

  • Book mover; hold alternate date if possible.
  • Order boxes, tape, bubble wrap, dish packs.
  • Notify landlord; clarify deposit rules.

Four weeks before

  • Submit crew ID lists to compounds that require them.
  • Confirm elevator and loading reservations.
  • Schedule utilities; screenshot meter readings.
  • Back up computers; secure password exports.

Three weeks before

  • Medical refills for two extra weeks.
  • School transcripts if changing institutions.
  • Pack guest rooms and storage first.

Two weeks before

  • Label by destination room; fragile on two sides.
  • Reconfirm crew count, truck size, start time.
  • Plan meals to empty freezer.
  • Arrange childcare or pet care for loading hours.

One week before

  • Suitcase per person with one week of clothes.
  • Open First box: tools, chargers, toilet paper, wipes, meds.
  • Confirm parking passes match ID spelling.
  • Withdraw small cash if using tips.

48 hours before

  • Reconfirm ETA and supervisor phone.
  • Strip beds; keep one linen set handy.
  • Charge devices; power strips in personal bags.
  • Photo each room for deposit disputes.

Night before

  • Defrost fridge if moving; wipe dry.
  • Disconnect washer; secure drum if required.
  • Sleep early; buffer alarms.

Moving day morning

  • Fragile walkthrough once with crew lead.
  • Door monitor while doors stay open.
  • Valuables off the truck.
  • Final closet, balcony, and cage sweep.

At destination

  • Verify labels before unload order.
  • Photo damaged cartons before opening.
  • Assemble beds early if kids need sleep.
  • Test appliances when power is live.

First week after

  • Change filters if dusty move.
  • Update address on banks and delivery apps.
  • Retain invoices for reimbursement.

Paperwork bundle

Single folder: estimates, amendments, payments, completion notes.

If plans break

Pause, photograph, call supervisor. Annotate paperwork with exceptions rather than refusing signatures without context.

Merging households

Unify box numbering systems before movers arrive—duplicate numbers cause wrong-room drops.

Industrial or home office gear

Label server and cable runs with photos; move confidential files in sealed totes you track personally.

Conclusion

Top movers succeed when clients treat relocation as a project. This checklist is the spine—execute it and most chaos disappears.
author: Active Movers Team
